creator: shailend@google.com
text:
I reproduced this with invariants+witness+ddb, it takes much longer to hit the deadlock due to lowered throughput due to invariants and witness. Backtraces of locked driver threads:  

```
[root@FreeBSD14 ~]# for locktid in $(procstat -ta | grep gve | grep lock | cut -d" " -f6); do procstat -k $locktid | grep $locktid; done
    0 100413 kernel              gve0 rxq 0          mi_switch __mtx_lock_sleep __mtx_lock_flags cache_alloc cache_alloc_retry tcp_m_copym tcp_default_output tcp_do_segment tcp_input_with_port tcp_input ip_input netisr_dispatch_src ether_demux ether_nh_input netisr_dispatch_src ether_input tcp_lro_flush tcp_lro_rx_done 
    0 100414 kernel              gve0 rxq 1          mi_switch __rw_wlock_hard _rw_wlock_cookie _inp_smr_lock in_pcblookup_hash_smr in_pcblookup_mbuf tcp_input_with_port tcp_input ip_input netisr_dispatch_src ether_demux ether_nh_input netisr_dispatch_src ether_input tcp_lro_flush tcp_lro_rx_done tcp_lro_flush_all gve_rx_cleanup_tq_dqo 
    0 100415 kernel              gve0 rxq 2          mi_switch __mtx_lock_sleep __mtx_lock_flags cache_alloc cache_alloc_retry tcp_m_copym tcp_default_output tcp_do_segment tcp_input_with_port tcp_input ip_input netisr_dispatch_src ether_demux ether_nh_input netisr_dispatch_src ether_input tcp_lro_flush tcp_lro_rx_done
```

Now in trying to find the resource loop, I initially failed because most driver threads are waiting on the uma zone lock (the lock that cache_alloc presumably needs) and this lock's address in the wchan column is 0xfffff800b877e9c0. But 0xfffff800b877e9c0 does not show up in the `show alllocks` output at all. However, there _is_ an owner for the uma zone lock there:

```
Process 857 (iperf) thread 0xfffff800b87ca000 (100719)
exclusive sleep mutex mbuf (UMA zone) r = 0 (0xfffffe00df57a3d0) locked @ /usr/src/sys/vm/uma_core.c:701
exclusive sleep mutex so_snd (so_snd) r = 0 (0xfffff80016ff7ca0) locked @ /usr/src/sys/netinet/tcp_output.c:350
```

In holding this lock, this iperf thread is probably holding up the driver threads. I'm not sure what to make of the fact that the addresses dont match: 0xfffffe00df57a3d0 (iperf) vs 0xfffff800b877e9c0 (gve). Especially given that we know both iperf and gve are getting at the mbuf zone.
